title: At the Restaurant
description: Order food, ask about the menu, and handle the bill politely in a Japanese restaurant.
situation: Eating out: being seated, ordering, requesting recommendations, the meal rituals, and paying.

phrases.tip: Always say this before a meal; it thanks everyone who made the food possible.

phrases.native: メニューをお願いします

phrases.tip: Point at the menu and say this; これ (kore) means 'this'.

phrases.native: ベジタリアンの料理はありますか
phrases.transliteration: bejitarian no ryouri wa arimasu ka
phrases.pronunciation: beh-jee-tah-ree-an noh ryoh-ree wah ah-ree-mahs kah

phrases.native: アレルギーがあります
phrases.tip: Name the food first, e.g. 卵 (tamago, egg) があります.

phrases.native: すみません、注文をお願いします
phrases.english: Excuse me, I'd like to order

phrases.native: お会計をお願いします
phrases.tip: In Japan you usually pay at the front counter, not at the table.
Say this after finishing; it is the bookend to いただきます.
Many smaller places are still cash-only, so it pays to ask.
